Tests for bitwise shift operations in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a set of assertions that test bitwise shift operations in JavaScript. It verifies the correctness of left shift (`<<`), signed right shift (`>>`), and unsigned right shift (`>>>`) operations. The assertions check specific cases, such as shifting the number 9 left by 2 bits to equal 36, and shifting 14 left by 2 bits to equal 56. It also tests right shifts, ensuring that 9 right-shifted by 2 bits equals 2, and -14 right-shifted by 2 bits equals -4. Additionally, it confirms that the unsigned right shift of 9 by 2 bits produces the same result as the signed right shift.
